historical_facts.claim: AMMClawback allows issuers to claw back clawback-enabled tokens from AMM pools.
historical_facts.quote_or_summary: Known Amendments describes AMMClawback and frozen-token AMMDeposit changes.

historical_facts.claim: XLS-73 frames the feature as regulatory-compliance related.
historical_facts.quote_or_summary: The standard describes clawback behavior for AMM pools and compliance motivation.

safe_route_label.label: HOLD_FOR_CHALLENGE
safe_route_label.notes: Compliance clawback inside AMM pools deserves explicit validator challenge even if eventually enabled.

short_description: Issuer clawback semantics extended into AMM pools.

sources.summary: XLS-73 describes issuer clawback interaction with AMM pools and regulatory-compliance motivation.
XRPL validators vote on amendments; more than 80% support must hold for two weeks, and servers without enabled amendment code become amendment-blocked.
XRPL's public known-amendments inventory, including status, default vote, amendment IDs, and descriptions.

technical_change: Adds AMMClawback and changes AMMDeposit behavior for frozen tokens.
